name: openvpn
explanation: The OpenVPN integration is logs-only: there is no Agent metric check (the `datadog_checks/openvpn/` package contains only a version stub, no `check.py`). The spec defines a single `logs` template that listens on a user-supplied TCP/UDP port for syslog forwarded from the OpenVPN server. The README explicitly requires the user to (a) configure syslog forwarding on the OpenVPN Access Server side and (b) pick a free port on the Agent host. Neither piece of input is discoverable from the wire — the upstream system actively pushes to the Agent on a port the user chose. Similar to pattern N (DogStatsD/instrumentation-only): auto-config in the network-probe sense doesn't apply.
notes: Logs-forwarder integration with no Agent check. The configuration is a syslog listener port, not a probe target.
